summary: Small, broad decomp cleanup PR that converted many near-matches into matches, led by Kirby copied-special routines plus smaller fixes in game-mode, ground, item, trophy/list, and sysdolphin code. The decomp.dev bot reported 52 new matches and one unmatched-item improvement, raising GALE01 matched code by 0.32% / 12392 bytes. Changes were mostly expression, type, struct-field, stack-padding, and temporary-variable adjustments rather than large rewrites.

key_files.reason: Main matching work for Kirby Donkey Kong copied neutral special; decomp.dev listed six DK/Kirby functions reaching 100%, mostly through stack padding and callback-store expression changes.
Many one-byte Kirby neutral-special and related action matches through condition tweaks, field selection, PAD_STACK adjustments, Vec3 local ordering, and motion-state parameter correction.
Corrected Kirby copied Koopa special field initialization from specialn_kp_lowest_charge_graphic_size to specialn_kp_flame_scale.
Matched several Kirby copied Koopa/Sheik-like special functions by adding PAD_STACK sizes.
Matched Marth/Roy copied special logic by casting charge comparison and replacing a raw numeric motion-state assignment with ftKb_MS_FeSpecialAirNLoop.
Cleaned unnecessary temporaries, direct cmd_vars[3] assignment, NULL assignment, and added PAD_STACK for copied-special matches.
Matched ftCo_800D105C by using fp->x34_scale.y as a scoped local for x2C4 scaling instead of reusing temp_f1.
Replaced raw pointer arithmetic over TmData offset 0x524 with typed td->x524 array accesses, adding PAD_STACK(24).
Exposed TmData offset 0x524 as HSD_Text* x524[4] instead of opaque padding, enabling typed accesses in gm_18A5.c.
Changed un_80312834 to return char* and return the final buffer cursor; bot reported it as a new match.
Matched HSD_SisLib_803A6048 with chained assignment to two globals after HSD_MemAlloc.

what_changed: Converted 52 functions/items to new matches according to the decomp.dev bot report, with matched code increasing by +12392 bytes.
Focused heavily on Kirby copied-neutral-special files: ftKb_SpecialN.c, ftKb_SpecialNNs.c, ftKb_SpecialNPk.c, ftKb_SpecialNYs.c, ftKb_SpecialNZd.c, ftKb_SpecialNFx.c, and ftKb_Init.c.
Adjusted many stack layouts by adding, removing, or resizing PAD_STACK/local padding, including PAD_STACK(8), PAD_STACK(16), PAD_STACK(24), and PAD_STACK(4 * 12).
Replaced several artificial or noisy locals with direct expressions, such as fp2->fv.kb.xC0 = NULL, fp->cmd_vars[3] = new_var, and Ground* gp = gobj->user_data.
Corrected field references in Kirby data and fighter state, including specialn_kp_flame_scale instead of specialn_kp_lowest_charge_graphic_size and fp->fv.kb.hat.x8_b0 instead of fp->fv.kb.xF4_b0.
Changed ftKb_EatTurn_Anim to call Fighter_ChangeMotionState with anim rate 1.0f instead of 0.0f.
Changed ftKb_SpecialLw_IASA to call ftKirbyDmgInline2 rather than ftKirbyDmgInline.
Reworked DK copied-special callback clearing to assign through a fresh Fighter* fp2 = gobj->user_data scoped block before nulling pre_hitlag_cb and post_hitlag_cb.
Improved game-mode type information by replacing TmData padding at 0x524 with HSD_Text* x524[4] and then using td->x524 indexes directly in fn_80198C60.
Changed gm_801BEFA4 signature from s8 ckind to int ckind in both source and header.
Changed TyListState::x2A1 from u8 to s8 and un_80312834 from void to char* with a return value.
Made smaller matching fixes in ground files grfourside.c, gricemt.c, grkongo.c, and grvenom.c, item file it_2E5A.c, toy.c, hsd_3AA7.c, and sislib.c.
smart_moves: Used typed struct recovery instead of pointer arithmetic in gm_18A5.c: TmData::x524[4] made the code clearer and still matched.
Preferred semantically meaningful motion-state constants over raw numeric temporaries, replacing 0x217 with ftKb_MS_FeSpecialAirNLoop.
Recognized that exact codegen sometimes depends on introducing a short scoped local, such as scale_y in ftCo_800D105C or fp2 = gobj->user_data before clearing callbacks.
Removed fake temporaries and fake padding where no longer needed, but also added precise PAD_STACK where matching required it.
Synchronized source/header/type changes together for signature and struct changes, avoiding mismatches between gm_1BA8.c/gm_1BA8.h and tylist.c/tylist.h.
Targeted many near-100% functions with minimal edits, producing a large aggregate matching gain from small localized changes.
decomp_lessons: When a function is already very close, try local ordering, exact stack pad size, and scoped temporary lifetime before changing control flow.
Struct-field recovery can be both a documentation improvement and a matching tactic; replacing opaque padding with a typed array can remove casts while matching.
Use exact signedness and return types in headers and structs. Here, int vs s8, char* vs void, and s8 vs u8 changes affected matching.
For bitfields, verify the bit index carefully; grIceMt changed an UnkFlagStruct access from b7 to b0.
Raw constants that represent motion states should be replaced with named enum values once identified; this helps searchability and reduces magic-number drift.
Expression form matters: base_size + extra_size did not codegen the same as extra_size += base_size in hsd_803AC340.
Chained assignments to globals can be required to reproduce original store ordering, as in HSD_SisLib_803A6048.
Do not assume temporary reuse is harmless; ftCo_800D105C matched by using fp->x34_scale.y in a separate local for later scaling.
naming_conventions: Kirby copied-special files use ftKb_SpecialN plus copy-source suffixes such as Ns, Pk, Ys, Zd, and Fx.
Kirby motion-state constants use ftKb_MS_* names, for example ftKb_MS_FeSpecialAirNLoop and ftKb_MS_CaSpecialAirN.
Recovered struct fields at known offsets keep offset-style names when semantics are still unknown, such as TmData::x524 and TyListState::x2A1.
Data attribute names use descriptive specialn_* fields when known, such as specialn_kp_flame_scale and specialn_odds_lose_ability_on_hit.
Unknown functions retain address-style names such as ftKb_SpecialN_800F5D04, gm_801BEFA4, un_80312834, and HSD_SisLib_803A6048.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Add/remove/resize PAD_STACK declarations to tune stack frames in near-matching functions.
Reorder local declarations, e.g. Vec3 vel before Vec3 pos, to match stack allocation/order.
Use scoped blocks to force local variable lifetime and store ordering, as with fp2 = gobj->user_data and scale_y = fp->x34_scale.y.
Replace fake pad arrays or volatile pads when they are no longer required, such as removing u8 _[8] and volatile short pad2.
Use explicit casts in comparisons when required, e.g. (s32)(*charge * 30) in ftKb_MsSpecialAirNLoop_Anim.
Prefer direct constant indexing and NULL assignment over artificial intermediate variables when codegen allows it.
Chained assignment can match dual-global initialization better than storing through a named temporary.

Small expression rewrites can matter: extra_size = base_size + extra_size, nested if after subtracting grKg_804D6980->unk52, and passing item_gobj instead of NULL to it_802E614C all appeared in matching fixes.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported GALE01 matched code 55.12%, +0.32%, +12392 bytes, with 52 new matches.

Highlighted major new matches including ftKb_DkSpecialN_Anim, ftCo_800D105C, multiple ftKb_DkSpecialN variants, it_802E5F8C, HSD_SisLib_803A6048, ftKb_EatTurn_Anim, ftKb_MsSpecialAirNLoop_Anim, and tylist functions.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Look for other TmData padding regions that can be replaced by typed HSD_Text* arrays.
Audit Kirby copied-special hat fields for remaining uses of fp->fv.kb.xF4_b0 versus fp->fv.kb.hat.x8_b0.
Search for near-matches where callback clearing through gobj->user_data changes codegen compared with using an existing Fighter* local.
Review remaining tylist/trophy functions for return-type or signedness mismatches similar to un_80312834 and TyListState::x2A1.
Search sysdolphin/baselib global initialization functions for chained assignment opportunities.
